risk: multi-late-update for same dim key not covered by AC; reload order can flip final state
add AC fixing merge rule (last-write-wins by source_ts) for repeated late updates on same key
AC3 says fact-row update emits a delta event but does not constrain ordering across multiple late updates for one key; downstream consumers may apply out-of-order
spec a monotonically increasing sequence or source_ts on emitted delta and require consumers to honor it
30-day cutoff not cross-checked against current retention policy; quarantine table lifecycle also unspecified
confirm 30d with retention owner; document quarantine TTL and triage SLA
AC4 covers boundary ages (5d, 35d) but not multi-update same-key behavior; idempotency claim of AC3 is untested
extend DQ matrix with: 2x late updates same key, replay of same late record (idempotency)
actions: multi-late-update same dim key with deterministic merge rule
validate against retention policy; document quarantine TTL
sequence or source_ts on emitted delta; consumer ordering contract
body: Direct answers to the two open questions: (1) No — AC1 implies single-update semantics, multi-late-update same dim key is the most likely production path and is not specified. (2) 30d cutoff is plausible but unverified; needs cross-check with the retention/quarantine policy before lock-in.
